title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant risk of data encryption, service disruption, and potential data exfiltration. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can infiltrate development environments, propagate malicious code, and impact production systems, leading to operational downtime and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Conduct an immediate review of CI/CD pipelines to identify and remediate any unauthorized access or misconfigurations.
Implement robust monitoring and logging for cloud infrastructure to detect and respond to suspicious activities in real time.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored securely offline to facilitate recovery in case of a ransomware attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The Shariff for WordPress Shariff for WordPress plugin through 1.0.11 does not sanitize or escape...
description: The Shariff for WordPress Shariff for WordPress plugin through 1.0.11 does not sanitize or escape the shariff_infourl setting before outputting it in the frontend HTML via the generateshariff() function, which could allow high privilege users such as admin to perform Stored Cross-Site Scripting attacks even when the unfiltered_html capability is disallowed (for example in multisite setup).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9677.

title: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to...
description: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 9.2.2. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to enumerate sequential report IDs and download complete form submission data — including names, email addresses, phone numbers, postal addresses, payment details, and uploaded file paths — for

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12404.

title: The MaxButtons – Create buttons plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site...
description: The MaxButtons – Create buttons plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the 'view' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 9.8.5 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that execute if they can successfully trick a user into performing an action such as clicking on a link.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary web scripts via a reflected XSS attack, potentially leading to session hijacking, phishing, or unauthorized actions on behalf of the user. This could compromise sensitive data and user trust.
recommended_actions: Update the MaxButtons plugin to the latest patched version immediately if available. If no patch is available, consider disabling or removing the plugin temporarily.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests targeting the 'view' parameter.
Educate users and administrators about the risks of clicking on untrusted links, especially those that appear suspicious or unexpected.

title: The Paid Membership Plugin, Ecommerce, User Registration Form, Login Form, User Profile &...
description: The Paid Membership Plugin, Ecommerce, User Registration Form, Login Form, User Profile & Restrict Content  WordPress plugin before 4.16.17 does not verify that the user performing a subscription action owns the targeted subscription, allowing any authenticated user (Subscriber+) to cancel other users' active subscriptions via an Insecure Direct Object Reference.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10820.

title: The Invoice Generator plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation due to a missing...
description: The Invoice Generator plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation due to a missing capability check on the pravel_invoice_edit_account() AJAX action in versions up to, and including, 1.0.0. The handler is exposed via wp_ajax_nopriv_pravel_invoice_edit_account, accepts an attacker-controlled user_id and user_email from POST data, and calls wp_update_user() without verifying authentication, ownership, or a nonce. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to change the ema

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12415.

title: The HD Quiz plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in versions 2.2.0 to...
description: The HD Quiz plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in versions 2.2.0 to 2.2.1. This is due to missing or incorrect nonce validation on the hdq_validate_nonce function. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to delete or modify quizzes and questions, create new quizzes, and change plugin settings via a forged request granted they can trick a site administrator into performing an action such as clicking on a link.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13422.

title: The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to...
description: The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to generic SQL Injection via 'query[select]' Parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.5.5 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Sales Representative-level access and above, to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Sales Representative-level access or higher to exploit SQL Injection, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, data manipulation, or database compromise. This could result in significant data breaches or operational disruptions.
recommended_actions: Update the Groundhogg plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available or contact the vendor for a security update.
Restrict access to the plugin by limiting Sales Representative-level accounts and reviewing user permissions to ensure the principle of least privilege is enforced.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL Injection attempts as an interim measure.

title: The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to...
description: The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to generic SQL Injection via the 'search' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.5.5 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with marketer-level access and above, to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to extract sensit

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with marketer-level access or higher to exploit SQL Injection, potentially leading to unauthorized data access, data exfiltration, or database manipulation. This could compromise sensitive customer data, disrupt marketing operations, and damage organizational reputation.
recommended_actions: Update the Groundhogg plugin to the latest patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily if no patch exists.
Restrict marketer-level access to only trusted users and review user permissions to minimize potential abuse.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to monitor and block malicious SQL injection attempts.

title: The CodePeople Post Map for Google Maps plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site...
description: The CodePeople Post Map for Google Maps plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via 'cpm_point' Post Meta in all versions up to, and including, 1.2.6 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13335.

title: HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) is susceptible to a sensitive data exposure...
description: HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) is susceptible to a sensitive data exposure vulnerability which could allow an attacker to exploit application information to then attempt additional attacks and cause unknown behavior in the application.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) could expose sensitive application data, potentially enabling attackers to gather information for further exploitation, such as targeted attacks or unauthorized access to systems.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update from HCL that addresses CVE-2025-59868 as soon as it becomes available.
Conduct a security review of HTMO configurations and limit access to sensitive data by implementing least privilege principles.
Monitor network and application logs for unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts related to HTMO.

title: HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) is susceptible to vulnerabilities due to .NET Framework...
description: HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) is susceptible to vulnerabilities due to .NET Framework 4.5 being out of service.  Since .NET Framework 4.5 has reached end-of-life and no longer receives security updates, it may expose the application to publicly known security weaknesses through vulnerable third-party components.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The use of an outdated .NET Framework (4.5) in HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) exposes the application to known vulnerabilities, potentially allowing attackers to exploit security weaknesses. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or compromise of sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the .NET Framework to the latest supported version (e.g., .NET 6 or later) to ensure continued security updates and compatibility.
Conduct a comprehensive security assessment of the application to identify and mitigate any existing vulnerabilities introduced by the outdated framework.
Monitor for any active exploitation attempts related to CVE-2023-37524 and apply necessary patches or mitigations immediately.

title: The Ivory Search – WordPress Search Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross...
description: The Ivory Search – WordPress Search Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via 'menu_title' and 'menu_magnifier_color' Settings in all versions up to, and including, 5.5.15 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-11356.

title: A vulnerability exists in H.View IP cameras certificate-related upload interfaces allow...
description: A vulnerability exists in H.View IP cameras certificate-related upload interfaces allow authenticated users to store arbitrary file content to fixed, persistent filesystem locations without validating file type, structure, or size. This design omission enables the placement of unexpected or malformed data in locations intended for trusted certificate material, which could affect system integrity or behavior even after reboot.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56414.

title: The DMP-5000 file service exposes authenticated arbitrary file upload functionality. There are...
description: The DMP-5000 file service exposes authenticated arbitrary file upload functionality. There are exposed endpoints which allows authenticated users to upload files of any type without validation. No file extension filtering or content inspection is enforced which allows executable binaries and scripts to be accepted and written directly to the server.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the DMP-5000 file service allows authenticated users to upload malicious files, including executable binaries and scripts, without any validation. This could lead to remote code execution, data breaches, malware deployment, or complete server compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and implement file type restrictions to prevent unauthorized file uploads.
Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to monitor and block malicious file upload attempts.
Update the DMP-5000 service to the latest patched version or apply vendor-provided mitigations for CVE-2026-33560.

title: The DMP-5000 devices are shipped with a default administrative web account with weak...
description: The DMP-5000 devices are shipped with a default administrative web account with weak authentication controls, which are not required to be changed during initial configuration or operation. Using these accounts provides full system access.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31928.

title: Various versions of Daktronics Controller Firmware could allow authenticated and unauthenticated...
description: Various versions of Daktronics Controller Firmware could allow authenticated and unauthenticated remote users to escape the intended directory and enumerate arbitrary file system paths.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28701.

title: A vulnerability exists in H.View IP cameras that could allow an authenticated user to supply...
description: A vulnerability exists in H.View IP cameras that could allow an authenticated user to supply unsanitized XML fields to the device's certificate generation interface, which are incorporated into a backend certificate creation command without proper input validation. This may allow for command execution with elevated privileges during certificate generation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-55975.

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the patron restriction type administration page of...
description: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the patron restriction type administration page of Koha Library Management System through 25.11 allows an authenticated remote attacker with administrator privileges to inject arbitrary web scripts via the restriction type label (display_text field)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The XSS vulnerability in the Koha Library Management System allows an attacker with administrator privileges to inject malicious scripts, potentially leading to session hijacking, data theft, or unauthorized actions within the system. This could compromise the integrity and confidentiality of the system and its users.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update for the Koha Library Management System as soon as it becomes available.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the 'display_text' field to prevent malicious script injection.
Conduct a security review of user roles and permissions to ensure that only trusted individuals have administrator privileges.

title: A stack overflow in the AP4_Array<AP4_TrunAtom::Entry>::EnsureCapacity component of axiomatic...
description: A stack overflow in the AP4_Array<AP4_TrunAtom::Entry>::EnsureCapacity component of axiomatic-systems Bento4 before v1.8.9allows attackers to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) via a crafted MP4 file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Bento4 library allows attackers to exploit a stack overflow via a crafted MP4 file, potentially causing a Denial of Service (DoS) attack. This could disrupt services relying on the library for media processing or playback.
recommended_actions: Update to Bento4 version 1.8.9 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization for MP4 files to detect and block malicious payloads.
Monitor systems using Bento4 for unusual activity or crashes that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the item type administration page of Koha...
description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the item type administration page of Koha Library Management System through 25.11 allows an authenticated remote attacker with administrator privileges to inject arbitrary web scripts via the item type check-in message field (checkinmsg)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-50767.

title: CVE-2019-25763 - WordPress Ultimate Addons for Beaver Builder 1.2.4.1 contains an authentication bypass vulnerability
description: WordPress Ultimate Addons for Beaver Builder 1.2.4.1 contains an authentication bypass vulnerability that allows attackers to gain unauthorized access by exploiting the social media login form functionality. Attackers can submit a POST request to the admin-ajax.php endpoint with the uabb-lf-google-submit action, a valid administrator email address, and a valid nonce to obtain session cookies and authenticate as that user.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2019-25763.

title: CVE-2026-48939 - A vulnerability in the iCagenda extension for Joomla allows the upload of arbitrary files in the fil
description: A vulnerability in the iCagenda extension for Joomla allows the upload of arbitrary files in the file attachment feature, ultimately resulting in PHP code upload and execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-48939.

title: CVE-2026-48909 - SP LMS (com_splms) < 4.1.4 by JoomShaper deserializes user-controlled cookie data without validation
description: SP LMS (com_splms) < 4.1.4 by JoomShaper deserializes user-controlled cookie data without validation, enabling an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code on the server.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on the server, potentially leading to full system compromise, data breaches, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately update SP LMS (com_splms) to version 4.1.4 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to detect and block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough audit of server logs to identify any signs of exploitation or unauthorized access.

title: CVE-2026-48908 - A vulnerability in SP Page Builder for Joomla allows unauthenticated users to upload arbitrary files
description: A vulnerability in SP Page Builder for Joomla allows unauthenticated users to upload arbitrary files, ultimately resulting in the upload and execution of PHP code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-48908.

title: CVE-2026-12119 - The Simple File List plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized file operations due to a mis
description: The Simple File List plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized file operations due to a missing authorization check on the 'frontmanage' shortcode attribute in all versions up to, and including, 6.3.7. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to perform arbitrary file operations including deletion, move, folder creation, and download. An attacker can create a draft post containing the 'eeSFL' shortcode, render it via the post preview endpoint to harvest the nonce needed to authorize the operations, and then submit file operation requests that bypass the intended authorization checks in includes/ee-list-ops-bar-process.php.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12119.

title: CVE-2026-11912 - The Simple File List plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file modification due to insuff
description: The Simple File List plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file modification due to insufficient authorization checks in all versions up to, and including, 6.3.7. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to delete and modify files on the serve. This vulnerability is exploitable even when the administrator has not enabled the AllowFrontManage setting, because the is_admin() check unconditionally short-circuits the guard before that setting is evaluated.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-11912.

title: CVE-2026-11911 - The Simple File List plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to insufficie
description: The Simple File List plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to insufficient file path validation in the eeSFL_DeleteFile function in all versions up to, and including, 6.3.7. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to delete arbitrary files on the server, which can easily lead to remote code execution when the right file is deleted (such as wp-config.php). The simplefilelist_edit_job AJAX action is registered via wp_ajax_nopriv_, making it accessible without authentication, and the is_admin() guard that would otherwise restrict access is bypassed because is_admin() always returns true for requests to the admin-ajax.php endpoint.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-11911.

title: CVE-2026-9843 - The Database for Contact Form 7, WPforms, Elementor forms plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbi
description: The Database for Contact Form 7, WPforms, Elementor forms plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to insufficient file path validation in the view_page function in all versions up to, and including, 1.5.1. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to delete arbitrary files on the server, which can easily lead to remote code execution when the right file is deleted (such as wp-config.php). Successful exploitation requires an administrator to view or edit the poisoned form entry, at which point PHP's bracket parser reshapes the attacker-crafted JSON key to bypass the stored-path isset check and trigger deletion of the traversal-specified file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9843.

title: CVE-2026-9265 - Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions before 1.96 for Perl permits a heap OOB read in print_attribute UTF8
description:
Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions before 1.96 for Perl permits a heap OOB read in print_attribute UTF8STRING path.

print_attribute() copies a UTF8STRING ASN.1 attribute value into a heap buffer sized exactly to its declared length via strncpy, leaving no NUL terminator. Downstream callers run strlen() on the result and pass the inflated length to newSVpvn(), copying attacker-influenced adjacent heap bytes into a Perl scalar.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9265.

title: CVE-2026-56216 - Capgo before 12.128.2 contains a scope escalation vulnerability in the POST /functions/v1/apikey end
description: Capgo before 12.128.2 contains a scope escalation vulnerability in the POST /functions/v1/apikey endpoint that allows app-limited API keys to mint unrestricted keys by setting empty limits. Attackers with a compromised app-limited key can create an unrestricted key with org-wide access to resources like app listings and other protected endpoints.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56216.

title: CVE-2026-56215 - Capgo before 12.128.12 allows authenticated users to modify their mutable public.users.email to arbi
description: Capgo before 12.128.12 allows authenticated users to modify their mutable public.users.email to arbitrary addresses, which the SSO provisioning endpoint trusts as an account-merge key. Attackers can pre-position their account with a victim's corporate SSO email, causing the provision-user endpoint to merge the victim's SSO identity into the attacker-controlled account.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56215.

title: CVE-2026-56214 - Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an information disclosure vulnerability in Supabase PostgREST RPC end
description: Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an information disclosure vulnerability in Supabase PostgREST RPC endpoints is_trial_org and is_paying_org that allows unauthenticated attackers to enumerate organizations and disclose billing status using the public sb_publishable key. Attackers can invoke these endpoints to determine organization existence via distinguishable return values and identify paying customers for targeted profiling.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56214.

title: CVE-2026-56213 - Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the public.upsert_version_me
description: Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the public.upsert_version_meta SECURITY DEFINER function exposed via PostgREST RPC, allowing unauthenticated attackers to insert arbitrary rows into version_meta for any app_id. Attackers can exploit this by calling the RPC endpoint with a public anon key to poison storage metrics, causing persistent false data in dashboards and triggering incorrect alerts across victim applications.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56213.

title: CVE-2026-56212 - Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an authentication logic flaw: a user with permission to manage team o
description: Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an authentication logic flaw: a user with permission to manage team or organization security settings can enable mandatory two-factor authentication for all team members without first enabling 2FA on their own account. The application fails to verify the initiator's 2FA status before allowing the policy change, resulting in inconsistent security enforcement, potential administrative misuse, and lockout risk for team members.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56212.

title: CVE-2026-11551 - The Branda plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation via account takeover in all ve
description: The Branda plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation via account takeover in all versions up to, and including, 3.4.29. This is due to the plugin not properly validating a user's identity prior to updating their password. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to change arbitrary user's passwords, including administrators, and leverage that to gain access to their account.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-11551.
